title: Repository: /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public Task: sanity-check this integration strategy only. Proposed path: add a repo script that decides whether bare `kernel` should auto-launch the repo's 4-pane tmux surface, then make ~/bin/kernel delegate to it; keep `KERNEL_AUTO_4PANE=false` as...
summary_text: Confirmations: - Strategy is sound at a high level: keep `~/bin/kernel` as the repo-entry wrapper and let it delegate to repo-local logic when available, so behavior can be tuned without changing the global launcher contract. - Using `KERNEL_AUTO_4PANE=false` as an explicit recursion/opt-out flag is appropriate and aligns with existing env-driven launch controls already used in this repo (`KERNEL_AUTO_OPEN_LATEST`). - Injecting the flag in `scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-launch.sh` for legacy tmux launch paths is the right place to prevent regressions in any entrypoint that goes through runtime launch flow. Risks: - If `~/bin/kernel` is changed to blindly call repo-local logic, non-repo contexts (or repos lacking the new script) can fail; add a safe fallback to current `codex-prompt-launch kernel` behavior. - Keep opt-out semantics strict: the repo script must check `KERNEL_AUTO_4PANE` before deciding to auto-launch, and clear/propagate it when calling deeper launchers to avoid loops. - Ensure behavior parity with `codex-kernel` launch guard expectations on no-arg `kernel` (e.g., latest-run reopen logic), or user expectations on Mac mini may diverge from existing documented semantics. - Add/extend tests around bare `kernel` behavior (`test-k-shortcuts.sh` currently focuses on `k`) to lock in the new recursion path.
